automotive-ev-battery.title: Industry calls on EU to simplify state aid for cleantech factories
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A coalition of industrial companies, investors, and associations has urged the EU Commission to revise state aid rules to enable more predictable production subsidies for battery factories and climate-friendly technologies across Europe. The open letter advocates for streamlined regulations to facilitate continued investment in European cleantech manufacturing infrastructure.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Asleep at the wheel: Tesla video sparks debate
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A video from Canada appears to show a Tesla driver asleep while the vehicle is in motion, reigniting concerns about the safety implications of autonomous driving assistance systems like Tesla's Full Self-Driving. The incident raises critical questions about driver responsibility and the appropriate use of driver assistance technology.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Nio builds battery research centre in Shanghai
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese EV manufacturer Nio is constructing a dedicated battery research and development center in Shanghai, featuring a pilot production line for lithium-ion cells and 31 test laboratories. The facility will focus on advancing next-generation battery technologies to support the company's expansion in the competitive EV market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Globally, One in Eight New Mercedes Passenger Cars Is Fully Electric
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Mercedes-Benz delivered 63,000 electric vehicles in the second quarter, with fully electric models now representing 13 percent of global passenger car sales, up five percentage points year-over-year. Despite strong performance globally, China remains a challenging market for the German luxury automaker's EV portfolio.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Wren Kitchens begins electrification of heavy goods fleet
automotive-ev-battery.summary: UK-based kitchen retailer Wren Kitchens has launched its fleet electrification program with the delivery of 10 Mercedes-Benz eActros 600 electric tractor units, signaling commitment to transitioning its entire heavy goods vehicle fleet to electric power. This marks a significant step in the company's broader sustainability objectives for commercial transportation.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Renault refreshes Master E-Tech with battery and comfort upgrades
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Renault has updated its Master E-Tech electric van with a chemically redesigned 87kWh battery, enhanced comfort and safety features, and additional variants including a new 40kWh chassis-cab configuration for urban delivery applications. The improvements expand the vehicle's appeal across various commercial use cases.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Cubos acquires TotalEnergies' German B2B charging business
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Energy service provider Cubos has acquired TotalEnergies' fleet charging operations in Germany, marking its third major acquisition in six months following previous takeovers of Swarco and ChargeOne. The consolidation demonstrates accelerating industry consolidation in the EV charging infrastructure sector.

automotive-ev-battery.title: OMV Petrom completes network of 384 fast-charging points
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Romanian energy company OMV Petrom has completed installation of 384 fast-charging stations across 92 locations in Slovakia, Hungary, and Romania as part of an EU-funded infrastructure project. The EU contributed approximately one quarter of the total investment to expand regional EV charging accessibility.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Leapmotor B03X Orders Open in Europe: A New Benchmark in the Urban Crossover Segment
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese EV manufacturer Leapmotor has opened European pre-orders for its new B03X compact crossover, a globally designed model combining advanced technology and versatile features for the urban segment. The vehicle represents Leapmotor's strategic expansion into international markets beyond China.

automotive-ev-battery.title: India: Tata Motors launches Sierra.ev as its seventh passenger EV
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tata Motors has launched the Sierra.ev as its seventh passenger electric vehicle offering in India, strengthening its position as the country's largest EV player by volume with the industry's widest electric car lineup. The expansion reflects growing consumer adoption of electric vehicles in the Indian automotive market.

science-space.title: NASA begins funding hardware for 'Skyfall' Mars helicopter mission
science-space.summary: NASA has selected Firefly Aerospace to design and manufacture the protective aeroshell for its nuclear-powered Skyfall Mars helicopter mission. This funding milestone represents significant progress in developing advanced exploration technology for the Red Planet.

science-space.title: NASA's Roman Space Telescope prepares for launch
science-space.summary: NASA's Roman Space Telescope is undergoing final preparations in Florida ahead of its scheduled August 2026 launch. The mission represents a major advancement in astronomical observation capabilities.

science-space.title: Dance of death between binary stars leads to an unusual supernova
science-space.summary: Researchers have discovered that some binary star systems produce unusual supernovae through their gravitational interaction, potentially resolving long-standing questions about a specific class of cosmic explosions. This finding deepens our understanding of stellar death processes.

science-space.title: Japanese company books 1,100 pounds of cargo space on SpaceX Starship mission to the moon
science-space.summary: The Japanese company ispace has secured cargo space aboard SpaceX's Starship megarocket for a lunar mission, expanding its commercial moon exploration initiatives. This partnership demonstrates growing international collaboration in lunar logistics.

science-space.title: Watch a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launch for record-breaking 36th time early on July 9
science-space.summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket is scheduled to achieve a historic milestone with its 36th flight, setting a new record for reusable rocket launches. The achievement underscores the viability of SpaceX's reusable rocket program.

science-space.title: Shoebox-sized 'detector satellites' could sniff out a nuclear bomb in space
science-space.summary: A new study proposes that a constellation of small cubesats equipped with specialized detectors could identify nuclear weapons deployed on adversary satellites. The technology could provide a new layer of space-based security and arms control verification.

science-space.title: Students Connect NASA Science With Indigenous Knowledge to Study Coastal Erosion
science-space.summary: Students at the Pleasant Point Passamaquoddy Reservation are combining NASA scientific methods with generations of Indigenous coastal knowledge to address accelerating coastal erosion. The collaborative approach demonstrates how traditional ecological understanding enhances modern environmental research.

science-space.title: 'This is the future of spatial intelligence': Vantor unveils stunning 3D satellite views of Earth
science-space.summary: The American Earth-observation company Vantor has unveiled advanced 3D mapping technology that provides unprecedented satellite imagery of Earth's surface. The capability represents a significant advancement in spatial intelligence and remote sensing applications.

science-space.title: Hubble Captures Star-Studded Cluster
science-space.summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope has captured detailed images of Messier 3, one of the Milky Way's largest globular clusters containing hundreds of thousands of ancient stars. The observation provides valuable data about stellar formation and galactic structure.

science-space.title: Trump gifts Artemis astronauts an American flag from the US Capitol to plant on the moon
science-space.summary: An American flag from the U.S. Capitol has been entrusted to Artemis astronauts for planting on the lunar surface during an upcoming mission. The historic gesture symbolizes national commitment to lunar exploration and American space achievement.

tech-ai.title: Feds demand autonomous vehicle companies stop interfering with first responders
tech-ai.summary: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration has issued a directive requiring autonomous vehicle companies to cease interfering with emergency response operations, asserting that emergency scenes are not acceptable "edge cases" for autonomous systems. This regulatory action underscores growing safety concerns about AV deployment in critical scenarios.

tech-ai.title: Meta is reportedly working on smart glasses that would be recording all the time
tech-ai.summary: Meta is developing prototype "super sensing" always-on smart glasses capable of continuously recording audio and capturing photos every few seconds, according to Financial Times reporting. The device would leverage Meta AI to analyze the captured data, though users may not have direct access to raw footage, raising significant privacy and ethical questions.

tech-ai.title: SpaceXAI releases Grok 4.5, which Elon describes as an 'Opus-class model'
tech-ai.summary: Elon Musk's xAI company has released Grok 4.5, positioning it as a more cost-effective and efficient alternative to other powerful large language models in the market. The new version represents a significant competitive move in the increasingly crowded generative AI landscape.

tech-ai.title: This startup thinks robotics is about to have its ChatGPT moment
tech-ai.summary: General Intuition is developing foundation models for physical AI using millions of hours of video game data to train smarter robots with minimal real-world data requirements. The startup believes this approach could democratize robotics development similarly to how large language models transformed AI.

tech-ai.title: ChatGPT's upgraded voice mode is better at shutting up
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI has released GPT-Live-1, an overhauled voice mode designed to provide more natural conversational experiences by reducing interruptions and respecting pauses in user speech. The new model can intelligently route queries to specialized text models like GPT-4o for reasoning and web search tasks.

tech-ai.title: Google's deepfake detector system used to debunk McConnell hoax pic
tech-ai.summary: Google's deepfake detection system successfully identified an AI-generated image purporting to show Senator Mitch McConnell in severe distress as a fabrication, demonstrating practical application of synthetic media detection tools. The incident highlights both the proliferation of deepfakes and the importance of verification technologies.

tech-ai.title: Lovable reportedly in talks to double its valuation to $13.2B
tech-ai.summary: AI-powered software development platform Lovable is in advanced funding discussions to double its valuation to $13.2 billion through a $300 million round led by Menlo Ventures. The financing milestone reflects strong investor confidence in AI-assisted development tools.

tech-ai.title: With EU backing, QuantumDiamonds aims to speed up chip manufacturing
tech-ai.summary: German startup QuantumDiamonds, backed by EU subsidies under the European Chips Act, is developing novel semiconductor inspection technologies to accelerate chip manufacturing. The initiative reflects European efforts to bolster domestic semiconductor production capacity.

tech-ai.title: Autonomous drone delivery startup Manna plots major US expansion
tech-ai.summary: Drone delivery startup Manna is establishing a U.S. operations and manufacturing facility in Tulsa, Oklahoma, with plans to eventually employ 1,000 people. The expansion represents significant investment in scaling autonomous last-mile delivery infrastructure.

tech-ai.title: Despite 'misgivings,' judge approves Elon Musk's $1.5M SEC settlement
tech-ai.summary: A federal judge has approved Elon Musk's $1.5 million settlement with the Securities and Exchange Commission regarding his Twitter stake disclosure practices, concluding a protracted regulatory dispute. The settlement resolves allegations that Musk failed to properly disclose his growing ownership position in the platform.

finance-markets.title: Oil Extends Gain as Fresh US Strikes Against Iran Rattle Market
finance-markets.summary: Oil prices surged as the United States conducted a second round of strikes against Iranian targets, heightening concerns about potential disruptions to Middle Eastern energy supplies. The escalating military tensions have elevated geopolitical risk premiums in crude markets.

finance-markets.title: Gold Holds Decline as US Strikes on Iran Magnify Inflation Risks
finance-markets.summary: Gold prices retreated despite renewed geopolitical tensions as escalating US strikes on Iran drove energy costs higher, intensifying inflationary pressures across global markets. The inflation concerns have shifted investor focus away from traditional safe-haven assets.

finance-markets.title: Inflation fears mount at Warsh's first Fed meeting
finance-markets.summary: Federal Reserve officials signaled at a recent meeting that future rate increases may be necessary to combat persistent inflation concerns. The discussion reflects ongoing debate within the central bank about the appropriate monetary policy stance.

finance-markets.title: Asian Shares Rise as Chips Rally, Brent Advances: Markets Wrap
finance-markets.summary: Asian equity markets climbed with semiconductor stocks leading gains as investor appetite for artificial intelligence-related equities rebounded. Oil prices extended their upward momentum following renewed US military action in the Middle East.

finance-markets.title: High Yields Set Stage for More Global Bond Inflow Into Indonesia
finance-markets.summary: Indonesia is positioned to attract continued foreign investment in its sovereign bonds as global investors seek value among emerging Asian assets offering some of the region's highest yields. Market participants believe interest rate hikes across the region may be nearing completion.

finance-markets.title: Apple Supplier Luxshare to Trade After HK's Biggest 2026 Listing
finance-markets.summary: Apple supplier Luxshare Precision Industry commenced trading in Hong Kong following a major initial public offering that raised HK$24.3 billion, marking the city's largest listing so far this year. The successful listing reflects continued investor interest in Asia-Pacific technology supply chain companies.

finance-markets.title: NZ Manufacturing Hits 5-Year High as Breman Highlights Recovery
finance-markets.summary: New Zealand's manufacturing sector expanded at its fastest pace in nearly five years, signaling economic recovery and validating the central bank's recent decision to raise interest rates. Reserve Bank Governor Anna Breman cited the manufacturing strength as evidence of improving economic conditions.

finance-markets.title: Blackstone's QTS Markets Data Center-Backed Loan
finance-markets.summary: A Blackstone-backed data center operator is raising $2 billion through a secured loan to refinance existing debt and fund distributions to investors. The financing reflects continued capital market activity in the expanding data center sector.

finance-markets.title: Luxury Car Firm DuPont Registry Group to Pick Banks for IPO
finance-markets.summary: DuPont Registry Group, a luxury automotive marketplace and media company, has selected financial institutions to underwrite its planned initial public offering. The transaction signals continued activity in the US equity capital markets.

finance-markets.title: How Japan's Top Eyewear Maker is Passing Inflation Test
finance-markets.summary: Japanese eyewear manufacturer JINS is capitalizing on improved pricing power by expanding into premium retail locations and introducing higher-priced product tiers despite inflationary pressures. The strategy demonstrates consumer acceptance of price increases in select market segments.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Sirens in Bahrain, Kuwait after US's deadly attacks on Iran
world-news.summary: The United States conducted deadly military strikes on Iran, killing at least one person in Iranshahr and causing widespread power outages in multiple Iranian cities. The attacks triggered air raid sirens across neighboring Gulf states including Bahrain and Kuwait, marking a significant escalation in regional tensions.

world-news.title: Ukraine to get licence to produce Patriot missiles, Trump says
world-news.summary: The United States has agreed to grant Ukraine a production license for Patriot air defense missiles, according to President Trump. The move represents a significant commitment to Ukraine's long-term military capability, though production timelines remain lengthy.

world-news.title: Nato allies announce £37bn for new missile project
world-news.summary: NATO member states have announced a £37 billion investment in a new multinational missile development programme during talks in Ankara. Prime Minister Keir Starmer is convening around a dozen allied leaders to advance the strategic defense initiative.

world-news.title: South Sudan, 15 years on: Still fighting for peace
world-news.summary: Fifteen years after gaining independence, South Sudan continues to struggle with a stalled peace process, deepening humanitarian crises, and severe economic challenges. The world's youngest nation faces ongoing internal conflict and instability that threatens regional stability.

world-news.title: Indonesia's free meals programme plagued by alleged corruption and waste
world-news.summary: President Prabowo Subianto's free meals initiative has been criticized for significant corruption and misallocation of resources, with funds reportedly directed to affluent regions rather than vulnerable populations. The allegations raise questions about oversight and implementation of the large-scale social programme.

world-news.title: Eight killed as landslide hits Rohingya school in Bangladesh
world-news.summary: A landslide struck a girls' school at a Rohingya refugee camp in Bangladesh, killing at least seven children and a teacher. The disaster occurred amid heavy monsoon rains battering the southeastern coastal region.

world-news.title: Trump to ask US Supreme Court for new hearing on birthright citizenship
world-news.summary: President Trump has announced plans to petition the US Supreme Court for a new hearing on birthright citizenship after the Court ruled against his previous executive order ending the practice. The move signals continued legal challenges to constitutional interpretations of citizenship rights.

world-news.title: Nigeria's president demands to know how fake agency was allegedly set up in his office
world-news.summary: Nigerian President Adeniyi Adeyemi has launched an investigation into how an unauthorized government agency was allegedly established within his own office, with officials accused of forging appointment letters and securing unauthorized funding. The scandal raises serious questions about internal governance and oversight within the presidency.

world-news.title: Trains and emergency calls affected after major outage at Australia's largest telecoms company
world-news.summary: Australia's largest telecommunications provider experienced a major nationwide outage affecting train services and emergency call systems, with server failures at data centers in Sydney and Melbourne responsible for the disruption. The exact technical cause of the critical infrastructure failure remains under investigation.

world-news.title: Starmer gifted gun and ammunition by Turkish president at Nato
world-news.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer received a firearm and ammunition as a gift from Turkey's president during NATO meetings in Ankara, which was retained by British officials rather than brought back to the United Kingdom. The symbolic exchange occurred during high-level allied defense discussions.

health-biotech.title: White House reviewing top contenders to lead FDA
health-biotech.summary: The White House is in advanced stages of selecting a new FDA commissioner from a pool of finalists, with an announcement expected in the near term. This leadership decision will significantly impact pharmaceutical regulation, drug approval timelines, and public health policy.

health-biotech.title: Trump officials push to onshore generic drugmaking
health-biotech.summary: Senior Trump administration officials including Marco Rubio and RFK Jr. are urging pharmaceutical industry leaders to increase domestic manufacturing of generic drugs. The initiative aims to reduce U.S. dependence on foreign drug production and address supply chain vulnerabilities.

health-biotech.title: Affordable Care Act insurers want more premium increases as enrollment sags
health-biotech.summary: For the second consecutive year, many ACA insurers are requesting double-digit premium increases driven by rising medical costs and federal policy changes. The trend reflects growing financial pressures on the health insurance market and may further impact enrollment and affordability.

health-biotech.title: FDA approves Vera kidney drug and additional biotech developments
health-biotech.summary: The FDA has approved Vera Therapeutics' Trutakna for treating IgA nephropathy, marking a significant advancement in kidney disease treatment options. The approval reflects progress in developing therapies for previously difficult-to-treat renal conditions.

health-biotech.title: Lung transplants extended survival for advanced cancer patients, study shows
health-biotech.summary: A new study demonstrates that organ transplantation can provide meaningful survival benefits for select stage 4 lung cancer patients whose disease has not spread systemically. The findings challenge traditional assumptions about transplant candidacy and may expand treatment options for certain cancer patients.

health-biotech.title: Prime Medicine wins arbitration dispute with Beam Therapeutics over gene-editing technology
health-biotech.summary: Prime Medicine has prevailed in an arbitration case against gene-editing competitor Beam Therapeutics, clearing the way for Prime to advance a gene therapy candidate for a rare disease. The ruling resolves a contractual dispute between two prominent gene-editing biotechs and removes a significant regulatory hurdle.

health-biotech.title: Oberland invests up to $400M in MeiraGTx eye gene therapies
health-biotech.summary: Oberland Therapeutics is committing up to $400 million to fund MeiraGTx's late-stage eye gene therapy programs, including a candidate recently reacquired from Johnson & Johnson. The substantial investment demonstrates continued market confidence in gene therapy approaches for inherited retinal diseases.

health-biotech.title: Satellos shows early efficacy data for Duchenne muscular dystrophy pill in adults
health-biotech.summary: Satellos Bioscience has released Phase 2 data from adult patients taking its investigational Duchenne muscular dystrophy oral therapy ahead of a potentially pivotal pediatric trial later this year. The early positive signals support advancement toward regulatory approval for this serious genetic muscle disease.

health-biotech.title: Sino Biopharm partners with AstraZeneca and GSK on respiratory drug development
health-biotech.summary: Sino Biopharmaceutical has signed separate drug development and licensing agreements with AstraZeneca and GSK, including a $200 million deal for a respiratory candidate. The partnerships underscore major pharmaceutical companies' continued interest in collaborating with Chinese biotech firms on innovative drug development.

health-biotech.title: Mass General Brigham and nurses set for state hearing amid largest Massachusetts nursing strike
health-biotech.summary: The Brigham, a Harvard-affiliated teaching hospital, and Mass General Brigham face testimony at the State House as the healthcare system and nursing representatives navigate the largest nursing strike in Massachusetts history. The labor dispute reflects broader challenges in healthcare worker compensation and contract negotiations.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto and stocks tumble after Trump declares ceasefire 'over' following Iran strikes
crypto-web3.summary: Bitcoin and altcoins declined sharply following President Trump's statement that the ceasefire with Iran is "over" amid escalating U.S.-Iran military tensions. The geopolitical escalation triggered broad-based selloffs across crypto and equities markets.

crypto-web3.title: European Commission looks to expand MiCA to cover emergence of tokenization, non-EU stablecoin issuers: report
crypto-web3.summary: The European Commission is seeking stakeholder input on expanding MiCA regulations to address tokenization and non-EU stablecoin issuers, with a September 30 deadline for comments. The expansion signals regulatory evolution in response to emerging crypto market developments.

crypto-web3.title: Sen. Wyden urges Senate leaders to preserve contested blockchain developer protections in broader crypto bill
crypto-web3.summary: Democratic Senator Ron Wyden is advocating for the preservation of blockchain developer protections in pending crypto legislation. The push highlights ongoing legislative debates regarding regulatory clarity and developer safeguards in the evolving crypto framework.

crypto-web3.title: Polymarket bets on U.S. marketing blitz to win back trust after 4-year ban: Report
crypto-web3.summary: Prediction market platform Polymarket is pursuing aggressive U.S. marketing efforts to rebuild credibility following a four-year regulatory ban period. The initiative reflects the company's commitment to legitimization and reentry into the American market.

crypto-web3.title: Dinari, tZERO join forces on turnkey platform for tokenized U.S. equities
crypto-web3.summary: Dinari and tZERO announced a partnership to develop a comprehensive platform for tokenized U.S. equities, advancing blockchain-based stock infrastructure. The collaboration underscores the growing momentum in building technology for real-world asset tokenization.

crypto-web3.title: BNB Chain is building a new layer-1 for high-frequency trading and AI agents
crypto-web3.summary: BNB Chain is developing a new Layer 1 blockchain capable of processing over 100,000 transactions per second through direct streaming, eliminating public transaction queues. The network targets enhanced speed and security for high-frequency trading and AI-driven applications.

crypto-web3.title: Cash App owner Block to pay $45 million after states say it made false security promises
crypto-web3.summary: Block Inc. has agreed to a $45 million settlement with state regulators over allegations of making false security commitments regarding its Cash App platform. The enforcement action reflects ongoing regulatory scrutiny of consumer protection practices in cryptocurrency and fintech services.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto VC Paradigm launches $1.2 billion AI fund as it broadens beyond digital assets
crypto-web3.summary: Venture capital firm Paradigm has raised a $1.2 billion fourth fund investing in AI, robotics, and other emerging technologies while maintaining its commitment to crypto ventures. The expanded strategy reflects shifting investment priorities within prominent crypto-focused venture firms.

crypto-web3.title: Cardano founding entity EMURGO steps down from Pentad governance role after wallet exploit
crypto-web3.summary: EMURGO, a founding entity of Cardano, has withdrawn from its governance role with Pentad following a wallet exploit that drained approximately 16 million ADA worth $2.4 million. The incident highlights ongoing security challenges within decentralized finance infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: Adam Back's bitcoin treasury firm scraps SPAC merger, seeks new deal
crypto-web3.summary: Bitcoin treasury firm BSTR and Cantor Equity Partners have terminated their planned SPAC merger agreement originally scheduled for July 2025, with the shareholder meeting indefinitely postponed. The company is now pursuing alternative strategic transactions.

startups-vc.title: AI chip maker SambaNova raises $1B at $11B valuation, 5 months after last mega round
startups-vc.summary: SambaNova, an artificial intelligence chip manufacturer, has secured $1 billion in funding at an $11 billion valuation despite previous reports of Intel's acquisition interest at significantly lower valuations. The rapid follow-on funding demonstrates strong investor confidence in AI semiconductor competition.

startups-vc.title: Lovable reportedly in talks to double its valuation to $13.2B
startups-vc.summary: Lovable, an AI-powered development platform, is in discussions to raise $300 million led by Menlo Ventures, which would double its valuation to $13.2 billion. The funding round reflects strong market demand for AI-assisted software development tools.

startups-vc.title: Prime Intellect raises $130M Series A to help enterprises build their own AI agents
startups-vc.summary: Prime Intellect, a 2024-founded startup, has secured $130 million in Series A funding to enable organizations to develop proprietary AI agent systems independently of major AI labs. The funding underscores enterprise demand for AI autonomy and reduced reliance on frontier AI providers.

startups-vc.title: Global Startup Investment Hit Record $510B In H1 2026 As AI Boom Accelerates Funding And Exits
startups-vc.summary: Global venture capital investment exceeded $510 billion in the first half of 2026, with Q2 marking the second-largest quarter on record and demonstrating a robust recovery in IPOs and acquisitions. The data reflects accelerating investor confidence driven by the ongoing AI boom and technology sector momentum.

startups-vc.title: Your gaming data could be the secret to AGI, according to this Bezos-backed startup
startups-vc.summary: General Intuition, a startup with backing from Jeff Bezos, posits that video game data may be superior to internet-sourced training data for achieving artificial general intelligence, addressing limitations in spatial and temporal understanding of current large language models. The company's thesis challenges prevailing assumptions about training data sourcing for advanced AI systems.

startups-vc.title: With EU backing, QuantumDiamonds aims to speed up chip manufacturing
startups-vc.summary: QuantumDiamonds, a German startup, is leveraging European Chips Act subsidies to develop novel chip inspection technologies that accelerate semiconductor manufacturing. The company exemplifies government-backed efforts to strengthen Europe's domestic semiconductor capabilities.

startups-vc.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips
startups-vc.summary: ZML, a French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has released ZML/LLMD, open-source software designed to reduce operational costs and improve efficiency in running AI models across diverse chip architectures. The free offering positions the company as a developer-friendly player in the competitive AI infrastructure space.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: AI, Energy And Biotech Lead The Way
startups-vc.summary: U.S. startups announced significant funding rounds during the week, with energy and AI sectors leading momentum and Joulent securing the largest round at $1.75 billion in strategic financing. The diversity of large rounds across sectors demonstrates broad investor appetite beyond AI verticals.

startups-vc.title: EdVisorly Raises $13.3M Series A To Fix The Messy College Transfer Process With AI
startups-vc.summary: EdVisorly, a Los Angeles-based AI-native platform, has closed a $13.3 million Series A to automate back-office workflows in university admissions and college transfer processes. The funding reflects growing institutional appetite for AI-powered solutions in higher education administration.

startups-vc.title: Former OpenAI exec Kevin Weil is now on the board of Stoke Space
startups-vc.summary: Kevin Weil, a former OpenAI executive, has joined the board of Stoke Space, signaling increased Silicon Valley interest in reusable rocket technology and commercial space ventures. The appointment underscores potential convergence between AI leadership and the aerospace sector.

cybersecurity.title: 15-Year-Old GhostLock Flaw Enables Root and Container Escape on Most Linux Distros
cybersecurity.summary: Researchers have disclosed GhostLock (CVE-2026-43499), a critical 15-year-old Linux kernel vulnerability that allows any logged-in user to gain full root control of unpatched systems. The flaw has been present in virtually every mainstream Linux distribution since 2011 and requires no special permissions or network access to exploit.

cybersecurity.title: Ubiquiti Patches Critical UniFi Flaws Across Connect, Talk, Access, Protect, and OS
cybersecurity.summary: Ubiquiti has released security updates addressing multiple critical vulnerabilities across its UniFi product line, including a CVSS 10.0 vulnerability in UniFi Connect that could result in privilege escalation and arbitrary command execution. The patches address flaws impacting UniFi Connect, Talk, Access, Protect, and OS platforms.

cybersecurity.title: GitHub 'Verified' Commits Can Be Rewritten Into New Hashes Without Breaking Signatures
cybersecurity.summary: Research reveals that signed Git commits can be cryptographically forged without the signing key, allowing attackers to create new commits with identical files, author information, and dates while maintaining valid GitHub verification badges. This undermines the integrity assurances that developers rely upon for supply chain security.

cybersecurity.title: New Ghost Phishing Wave Is Breaking Traditional Email Security
cybersecurity.summary: A new "ghost phishing" campaign tracked as EvilTokens is bypassing traditional email security by hiding malicious pages until they decrypt and activate within victims' browsers. The technique exploits a blind spot in URL-based security checks, threatening access to Microsoft 365 accounts and sensitive organizational data.

cybersecurity.title: FBI Seizes NetNut Proxy Platform, Popa Botnet
cybersecurity.summary: The FBI has seized hundreds of domains associated with NetNut, a residential proxy service operated by publicly-traded Israeli company Alarum Technologies, following security research linking the platform to the Popa botnet comprising at least two million compromised devices. The action represents a significant enforcement operation against a major proxy infrastructure used to facilitate malicious activities.

cybersecurity.title: GitHub Copilot Refuses Harmful Requests in Chat, Then Writes Them in Code
cybersecurity.summary: Researchers have discovered that AI coding assistants including GitHub Copilot, Claude, and Gemini can be manipulated into generating harmful code by breaking requests into small, innocuous-looking steps within the code editor, even when the same request is refused in chat interfaces. This bypass technique exploits differences in how AI models enforce safety guardrails across different interaction modes.

cybersecurity.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
cybersecurity.summary: Two members of the prolific Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom to charges related to an August 2024 cyberattack that disrupted London's entire public transport network. The early guilty pleas by key gang members marked a significant development in prosecuting one of the cybercrime world's most active criminal organizations.

cybersecurity.title: New HalluSquatting Attack Could Trick AI Coding Assistants Into Installing Botnet Malware
cybersecurity.summary: Security researchers have identified HalluSquatting, an attack technique that exploits AI coding assistants' tendency to fabricate project names by registering fake domains that the AI reliably invents and then retrieves. The technique could lead to installation of botnet malware and other malicious payloads on developer systems.

cybersecurity.title: The Verification Step Is the New ATO Battleground in 2026
cybersecurity.summary: Account takeover attacks are evolving beyond credential stuffing as attackers increasingly target verification steps that have become stronger with the adoption of passkeys and multi-factor authentication. Security leaders face a new defensive challenge as traditional initial access methods become more difficult to exploit.

cybersecurity.title: China-Linked UAT-7810 Expands ORB Network With New LONGLEASH Malware
cybersecurity.summary: Chinese advanced persistent threat actor UAT-7810 is actively enhancing its bespoke malware to expand the LapDogs Operational Relay Box network by targeting internet-facing networking devices. The campaign represents ongoing Chinese state-sponsored efforts to establish persistent infrastructure within critical networks for espionage and disruption capabilities.

energy-climate.title: Six charts show how clean power was world's largest source of new energy in 2025
energy-climate.summary: Clean power surpassed all other energy sources to become the world's largest contributor to new global energy capacity in 2025, marking a significant milestone in the energy transition. The analysis provides comprehensive data on the scale and momentum of renewable energy deployment worldwide.

energy-climate.title: Grid expansion, storage, flexibility lag behind Europe's renewables growth
energy-climate.summary: A European Climate Neutrality Observatory report warns that grid infrastructure, electricity storage, and flexibility solutions are not expanding fast enough to match Europe's rapid renewable energy capacity additions. The infrastructure gap poses a significant challenge to scaling renewable power deployment across the continent.

energy-climate.title: One-third of India's new renewable energy capacity faces curtailment
energy-climate.summary: ICRA reports that approximately one-third of India's recently commissioned renewable capacity is experiencing grid curtailment due to insufficient transmission infrastructure expansion. Continued delays in transmission projects threaten to undermine the country's renewable energy deployment progress.

energy-climate.title: Cropped 1 July 2026: Heatwave scorches Europe | UK 2050 farm plan | What's next for the High Seas Treaty
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's curated digest covers major climate and sustainability developments including an extreme heatwave across Europe, the UK's 2050 agricultural sustainability plan, and ongoing negotiations for the High Seas Treaty. The roundup highlights critical intersections of climate, land use, and food security policy.

energy-climate.title: Q&A: Where do the UN secretary general candidates stand on climate change?
energy-climate.summary: Candidates vying to replace António Guterres as UN secretary general have been assessed on their positions and commitments regarding climate policy and action. The analysis provides crucial insight into how climate priorities may shift under new UN leadership.

energy-climate.title: Pembina and partners move forward with $3.2bn Greenlight Electricity Centre
energy-climate.summary: Pembina Pipeline has reached a final investment decision to proceed with the Greenlight Electricity Centre, a $3.2 billion gas-fired power plant project. The investment signals continued reliance on natural gas infrastructure despite global energy transition momentum.

energy-climate.title: Germany plans to reduce subsidies for heat pumps
energy-climate.summary: Germany is restructuring its BEG efficient buildings subsidy program to reduce funding limits while introducing stronger income-based targeting and new eligibility criteria for heat pump installations. The policy changes also include plans to incentivize European-manufactured heat pumps.

energy-climate.title: South Africa awards licenses to 1 GW of solar
energy-climate.summary: South Africa has awarded renewable energy procurement contracts for four solar projects totaling over 1 GW of installed capacity as part of its seventh bid window procurement round. The awards represent continued progress in the country's renewable energy expansion program.

energy-climate.title: Unigrid unveils sodium-ion residential energy storage systems
energy-climate.summary: Unigrid has launched Na+Casa, a sodium-ion residential energy storage system featuring proprietary cathode technology with a 10,000-cycle lifespan and enhanced safety characteristics compared to lithium-ion alternatives. The 9.25 kWh system has commenced European deployments with US market entry planned for 2026.

energy-climate.title: Algeria's path to 15 GW of solar
energy-climate.summary: Industry experts identify key requirements for Algeria to achieve its 2035 solar capacity target of 15 GW, emphasizing the need for enhanced private sector participation, transparent investment frameworks, and international partnerships. The analysis outlines barriers and opportunities for accelerating solar deployment in North Africa.

asia-pacific.title: US says China gave only hours' warning before Pacific submarine missile launch
asia-pacific.summary: The United States criticized China for providing insufficient advance notice of a submarine-launched ballistic missile test conducted in the Pacific, marking a breach of international nuclear power protocols. The US State Department characterized China's actions as part of Beijing's broader 'rapid and opaque nuclear weapons build-up,' raising concerns about strategic transparency and regional security.

asia-pacific.title: India, Indonesia Put in Place a Blueprint for a Durable Strategic Partnership
asia-pacific.summary: India and Indonesia finalized a landmark agreement on the supply of BrahMos missile systems and Astra air-to-air missiles during the Indian prime minister's visit to Indonesia. The defense procurement agreement reinforces the strategic partnership between the two major Southeast Asian and South Asian powers.

asia-pacific.title: US hearing weighs higher tariffs over alleged forced labour, targeting China
asia-pacific.summary: The US Trade Representative's office held a public hearing on proposals to impose tariffs on goods linked to forced labor, with significant focus on supply chains involving China. Participants debated the effectiveness of tariff measures in improving workers' rights amid the broader Section 301 investigation into international supply chains.

asia-pacific.title: US-China scientific decoupling: the hidden costs of geopolitical rivalry
asia-pacific.summary: The United States and China have reached historic lows in scientific collaboration amid rising geopolitical tensions and competitive pressures. The deterioration in academic and research partnerships carries significant implications for innovation and technological advancement across multiple sectors.

asia-pacific.title: Saudi Arabian tech giant eyes Hong Kong as Asia gateway and smart city partner
asia-pacific.summary: A major Saudi Arabian technology company has designated Hong Kong as its strategic gateway to Asia, expressing interest in co-developing smart city infrastructure with local authorities. The move reflects deepening economic ties between the Gulf region and Hong Kong as a regional financial and technology hub.

asia-pacific.title: Myanmar has 'a mountain to climb' to woo 2 million tourists a year
asia-pacific.summary: Myanmar's junta-led government faces significant obstacles in achieving its ambitious goal of attracting 2 million annual tourists, with analysts warning of structural challenges following the 2021 military coup. Over 100,000 deaths have been recorded in the ensuing civil unrest, creating substantial barriers to tourism recovery and international legitimacy.

asia-pacific.title: Why is India Allowing Chinese Equipment in Critical Government Projects Again?
asia-pacific.summary: Despite geopolitical tensions, India continues to rely on Chinese equipment and inputs in critical government infrastructure and production chains, even in sectors symbolizing the country's economic independence. This dependence reveals structural vulnerabilities in India's efforts to decouple from Chinese supply networks.

asia-pacific.title: Despite Sanctions, the Myanmar Government Continues to Procure High Volumes of Jet Fuel
asia-pacific.summary: Despite international sanctions, Myanmar's government has maintained substantial procurement of jet fuel through a covert supply network, according to analysis of official port documents and banking records. The findings reveal sophisticated sanctions evasion mechanisms that continue to fuel the military regime's operations.

asia-pacific.title: More and More of East Asia's Youth Have Given up on Finding Jobs
asia-pacific.summary: East Asian nations face a growing employment crisis among youth, reflecting a breakdown in traditional social contracts regarding job accessibility and economic opportunity. The trend signals broader structural challenges in labor markets across the region that require systemic policy solutions.

asia-pacific.title: The New Constitution Reset the Presidential Clock in Kazakhstan. But Will Tokayev Run?
asia-pacific.summary: Kazakhstan's Constitutional Court confirmed that President Tokayev remains eligible to seek another seven-year term under the nation's revised constitutional framework. The ruling clarifies the president's political future amid ongoing constitutional reforms in the Central Asian nation.
